title: Hands-on-Keyboard Intrusion (Linux)
briefing: Host $TARGET shows a successful SSH login for $USER1 from $SRC1, immediately followed by privilege escalation and command activity: a sudo to root, a payload pulled from the internet, and an outbound reverse shell. An operator is live on the keyboard. Reconstruct the sequence and prioritise the response.
dashboard_hint: Pivot on agent.name: $TARGET and sort by @timestamp. Chain: rule.id 5715 (Accepted password) -> 5402/5403 (sudo, group sudo) -> 100212 (curl|wget piped to a shell, full_log has the command) -> 100211 (/dev/tcp reverse shell). Read full_log on the 1002xx alerts — the command is the evidence.

questions.explain: Login then sudo, download-and-run, and a reverse shell in quick succession is a live operator, not automation.
All of the activity lands on $TARGET; the other hosts are unaffected.

questions.prompt: Which stages did the actor perform on $TARGET? (select all)
options: Successful SSH login with valid creds
Privilege escalation via sudo to root
Downloaded and executed a remote payload
Opened a reverse shell / C2 channel
answer: Successful SSH login with valid creds
Privilege escalation via sudo to root
Downloaded and executed a remote payload
Opened a reverse shell / C2 channel
questions.explain: 5715 login, 5402/5403 sudo, 100212 ingress, 100211 reverse shell. No ransomware or Kerberos activity was present.

questions.prompt: Which single event is the highest-severity indicator?
options: The reverse shell / outbound C2 channel
questions.answer: The reverse shell / outbound C2 channel
questions.explain: A reverse shell is active, root-capable, attacker-controlled access — the most urgent to cut.

questions.prompt: Source IP the operator came from?
questions.explain: The 5715 login carries data.srcip = $SRC1; the on-host commands have no srcip.
Valid creds, sudo privesc, tool ingress, reverse shell — no encryption/impact stage occurred.

options: Block the C2 / reverse-shell destination and $SRC1 egress+ingress
Rotate $USER1 and any root/secret material on the host
Just delete the downloaded file and move on
answer: Block the C2 / reverse-shell destination and $SRC1 egress+ingress
Rotate $USER1 and any root/secret material on the host
questions.explain: Root was reached and a live channel is open — isolate, cut C2, rotate creds, and preserve evidence. Deleting one file leaves the reverse shell and persistence intact.
